title: Short-run production costs
description: Practice what you have learned about short-run production costs in this exercise.
topic_path: Production and costs in the short run

choices.content: up to the $3^{\text{rd}}$ unit of output
between $6$ and $8$ units of output
between $4$ and $5$ units of output
over none of these quantities
over all of these quantities

assessment_items.question_content:
Ulterior Votives, LLC produces candles in a perfectly competitive market. It has a production function that exhibits diminishing marginal product. 

**Which of the following best describes this firm’s short-run marginal cost curve $(MC)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $MC$ slopes upward as output increases.
$MC$ slopes downward and becomes steeper as output increases.
$MC$ is constant for all quantities produced.
$MC$ is horizontal.
$MC$ slopes downward as output increases for all quantities produced.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fantabulous Enterprises uses capital and labor to produce its good. Its only variable input is labor. The firm’s marginal cost $(MC)$ and average total cost $(ATC)$ are shown in this graph. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/566c38f4a7081d069e096899d9b027fe4f5d034c)

**Which of the following explains the slope of $MC$ when the firm produces a smaller quantity than $3$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The firm’s marginal product of labor increases in this range of output.
The firm’s marginal product decreases in this range of output.
The firm’s marginal product of labor is maximized in this range of output
The firm’s average product of labor decreases in this range of output.
The firm’s average product of labor is constant in this range of output.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tuma Unlimited produces noodles in a perfectly competitive market. Its average total costs, marginal costs, and average variable costs have all decreased, but its average fixed costs haven't changed.

**Which of the following would cause these changes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an improvement in productivity
an increase in fixed costs
a tax on fixed costs
a subsidy on fixed costs
a tax on variable costs

assessment_items.question_content:
Kananelo Enterprises’ average total cost curve $(ATC)$ has shifted up, but its average variable cost curve $(AVC)$ and marginal cost curve $(MC)$ haven't changed. 

**Which of the following would cause the change in $ATC$, but not affect $AVC$ or $MC$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a tax on a fixed cost of production
a tax on a variable cost of production
an improvement in productivity
a decline in productivity
a decrease in average fixed costs

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that a firm’s only variable input is labor and a new tax has been placed on labor. 

**Which of the following correctly describes the impact on each of:  the marginal cost curve $(MC)$, the average variable cost curve $(AVC)$, average total cost curve $(ATC)$, and average fixed cost curve $(AFC)$?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: $MC$ shifts up; $AVC$ shifts up; $ATC$ shifts up; $AFC$ doesn’t change.
$MC$ doesn’t change; $AVC$ doesn’t change; $ATC$ shifts up; $AFC$ shifts up.
$MC$ doesn’t change; $AVC$ doesn’t change; $ATC$ shifts up; $AFC$ doesn’t change.
$MC$ doesn’t change; $AVC$ shifts up; $ATC$ shifts up; $AFC$ doesn’t change.
$MC$ shifts up; $AVC$ doesn’t change; $ATC$ doesn’t change; $AFC$ doesn’t change

assessment_items.question_content:
Prawn Quixote is a food truck serving seafood baskets. It has total fixed costs of $\$300$ and its total variable costs are given in this table.

Quantity of seafood baskets | Total variable cost $(TVC)$
-|-
$0$| $\$0$
$1$ | $\$2$
$2$ | $\$8$
$3$ | $\$18$
$4$ | $\$32$
$5$ | $\$50$
$6$ | $\$72$
$7$ | $\$98$

**What is the marginal cost $(MC)$ of the fifth seafood basket?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the marginal cost of the fifth seafood basket you must first find the total cost of the 5th seafood basket and the total cost of the fourth seafood basket.
Total cost is the total fixed cost plus total variable cost:

$TC(Q) = TFC + TVC(Q)$
Total fixed costs are \$300 and total variable cost of the fourth unit are \$32:

$\begin{align}TC(4) &= TFC + TVC(4)\\\\
& =\$300 + \$32\\\\
&=\$332\end{align}$

Total fixed costs are \$300 and total variable cost of the fifth unit are \$50:

$\begin{align}TC(5) &= TFC + TVC(5)\\\\
& =\$300 + \$50\\\\
&=\$350\end{align}$
Marginal cost is the difference between the total cost of producing 5 units and the total cost of producing 4 units:

$MC(5) = TC(5) - TC(4)$
The marginal cost of the 5th unit is \$18:

$\begin{align}MC(5) &= TC(5) - TC(4)\\\\
&=\$350 - \$332\\\\
&=\$18\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Trawls Well is a food truck serving seafood baskets. It has total fixed costs $(TFC)$ of $\$300$ and its total variable costs $(TVC)$ are given in the table below.

Quantity of seafood baskets | TVC
-|-
$0$| $\$0$
$1$ | $\$2$
$2$ | $\$8$
$3$ | $\$18$
$4$ | $\$32$
$5$ | $\$50$
$6$ | $\$72$
$7$ | $\$98$

**What is the average total cost of the fifth seafood basket?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The average total cost of the fifth unit is the total cost of producing$5$ units divided by $5$.

Total costs are total fixed costs plus total variable costs:

$TC(5) = FC + VC(5)$
The total cost of producing $5$ units is $\$350$:

$\begin{align} TC(5) & = FC + VC(5)\\\\
&=\$300 + \$50\\\\
&=\$350\end{align}$
The average total cost $(ATC)$ of producing $5$ units is $\$70$:

$\begin{align}ATC(5) &=\dfrac{TC(5)}{5}\\\\
&=\dfrac{\$350}{5}\\\\
&=\$70\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Pie Claudius produces and sells pies. Its total cost of producing different quantities of pie are given in the table below.

Quantity of pies | Total cost
-|-
$0$| $\$400$
$1$ | $\$420$
$2$ | $\$435$
$3$ | $\$455$
$4$ | $\$480$
$5$ | $\$510$
$6$ | $\$545$


**What is the average variable cost of the fourth pie?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the average variable cost of 4 pies, you must first find the variable cost of 4 pies. The variable cost is the difference between total cost and fixed cost:

$\begin{align} \text{Total cost}(4) & =FC + VC(4)\\\\
\text{Total cost}-FC&=VC(4)\end{align}$
Fixed costs are equal to total costs when output is zero.
The total cost of producing $0$ pies is $\$400$, so the fixed costs are $\$400$.
The variable cost of producing 4 pies is $\$80$:

$\begin{align} \text{Total cost} -FC &=VC(4)\\\\
\$480 - \$400 & = \$80\end{align}$

Average variable cost is variable cost divided by quantity:

$AVC(4) = \dfrac{VC(4)}{4}$
The average variable cost of 4 pies is \$20:

$\begin{align}AVC(4) &= \dfrac{VC(4)}{4}\\\\
& =\dfrac{\$80}{4}\\\\
&=\$20\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true about the relationship between marginal cost $(MC)$ and average total cost $(ATC)$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: If $MC$ is less than $ATC$, then $ATC$ is decreasing.
If $MC$ equals $ATC$, then $ATC$ is at its maximum
If $MC$ is greater than $ATC$, then $MC$ is decreasing
If $MC$ is greater than $ATC$, then average variable cost and $ATC$ are moving farther apart
If $MC$ is less than $ATC$, then $ATC$ is minimized.

assessment_items.question_content:
A Thousand Splendid Buns sells steamed buns in a perfectly competitive market. The firm’s only variable input is labor and its costs curves are given in this table.

Quantity (in thousands) | Variable costs | Total cost | Marginal costs $(MC)$ | Average variable costs $(AVC)$
-|-|-|-
$0$ | $\$0$ | $\$400$ | - | -
$1$ | $\$1{,}000$ | $\$1{,}400$ | $\$1{,}000$ | $\$1{,}400$
$2$ | $\$1{,}900$ | $\$2{,}300$ | $\$900$ | $\$1{,}150$
$3$ | $\$3{,}000$ | $\$3{,}400$ | $\$1{,}100$ | $\$1{,}133$
$4$ | $\$5{,}000$ | $\$5{,}400$ | $\$2{,}000$ | $\$1{,}350$
$5$ | $\$9{,}000$ | $\$9{,}400$ | $\$4{,}000$ | $\$1{,}880$
$6$ | $\$14{,}000$ | $\$14{,}400$ | $\$5{,}000$ | $\$2{,}400$

**Which of the following statements is true based on the data in this table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The average product of labor decreases for quantities greater than $3{,}000$ buns.
The marginal product of labor is decreasing for all quantities.
The marginal product of labor is maximized when $3000$ buns are produced.
The average product of labor is decreasing when fewer than $2{,}000$ buns are produced.
The marginal product of labor is minimized when $2{,}000$ buns are produced.

assessment_items.question_content:
**When a firm’s only variable input is labor, the average variable cost $(AVC)$:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: is lowest when the average product of labor is highest.
is lowest when the marginal product of labor is highest.
rises when the average product of labor rises.
rises when the marginal product of labor rises.
falls when the average product of labor falls.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume there is a range of output over which there are gains from specialization and the division of labor.


**What must be true if average variable costs $(AVC)$ are increasing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Marginal costs $(MC)$ must be greater than $AVC$.
Average total costs $(ATC)$ must be increasing.
Marginal costs $(MC)$ must be decreasing.
Marginal costs $(MC)$ equals average total costs $(ATC)$.
As the amount of output increases, average total cost $(ATC)$ and $AVC$ get farther apart.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes why the marginal cost curve slopes upward?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Production functions with diminishing marginal returns yield upward sloping marginal cost curves.
Increasing returns to inputs create increasing costs.
People get less and less additional satisfaction from each unit of consumption.
The income effect overtakes the the substitution effect.
Specialization and the division of labor change the costs a firm faces.
